name: Flow
helpText:
Flow

The flow is the container for components and generally represents an atomic action.
allowableProperties_comment: Note that the order of properties in this file dictates the order displayed on screen, unless propertyDisplayOrder is set, which will be used as the primary key

name.helpText: The name of the component as displayed on diagrams, spaces are encouraged, succinct is best. The name should be unique for the flow. The name will be used internally to reflect the bean created.

configurationId.helpText: By default, the configuration ID = modulename + flowname + componentName + componentType. There are times when you may wish to override this i.e. if you want multiple flows to share the same configration or you have changed a flow name but don't want to update properties that may have already been stored in the override properties database.

flowStartupType.helpText:
flowStartupType controls how a flow behaves when the module starts up.

  • AUTOMATIC — the flow starts automatically when the module activates
  • MANUAL — the flow is not started automatically; it must be started by an operator via the dashboard/REST API
  • DISABLED — the flow cannot be started at all

During module activation, each flow’s StartupControl is checked: if it’s AUTOMATIC (and the flow wasn’t previously stopped), flow.start() is called. Otherwise it logs that the flow won’t be auto-started.

This configuration will set ikasan.module.activator.startup.type.defaultStartupType in application.properties. Additional manual properties include

Set a specific flow (format: flowName,StartupType,optionalComment)

ikasan.module.activator.startup.type.flowStartupTypes[0]=My Flow,MANUAL,Requires manual start

Prevent overwriting what an operator has set in the DB

ikasan.module.activator.startup.type.allowDbOverwrite=false

The key design intent is:

  1. DB is the source of truth at runtime — operators can change a flow’s startup type via the dashboard/REST API and that is persisted to the database. allowDbOverwrite=false (the default) ensures code/config cannot silently override an operator’s decision.
  2. Properties provide initial defaults — the properties are only applied when no DB value exists yet (first deployment) or if allowDbOverwrite=true is explicitly set.
  3. Setting it in code would bypass the persistence layer — if you hardcoded a StartupType in Java, you’d fight against the DB-driven state that the runtime and dashboard manage, making operator control unreliable.

Set a default for all flows

ikasan.module.activator.startup.type.defaultStartupType=AUTOMATIC

Set a specific flow (format: flowName,StartupType,optionalComment)

ikasan.module.activator.startup.type.flowStartupTypes[0]=My Flow,MANUAL,Requires manual start

Prevent overwriting what an operator has set in the DB

ikasan.module.activator.startup.type.allowDbOverwrite=false

The key design intent (from the BulkStartupTypeSetupServiceConfiguration javadoc) is:

  1. DB is the source of truth at runtime — operators can change a flow’s startup type via the dashboard/REST API and that is persisted to the database. allowDbOverwrite=false (the default) ensures code/config cannot silently override an operator’s decision.
  2. Properties provide initial defaults — the properties are only applied when no DB value exists yet (first deployment) or if allowDbOverwrite=true is explicitly set.
  3. Setting it in code would bypass the persistence layer — if you hardcoded a StartupType in Java, you’d fight against the DB-driven state that the runtime and dashboard manage, making operator control unreliable.
